stories.headline: Switzerland Rejects Proposal to Cap Population at 10 Million
stories.summary: Switzerland rejected a proposal to cap its population at 10 million people, as warnings about economic damage if the radical measure was introduced outweighed concerns about immigration.

stories.headline: Trump warns Netanyahu over Beirut strike as US-Iran peace deal hangs in the balance
stories.summary: Iranian state media reported on Sunday that Tehran has not yet reached a final decision on the proposed peace deal to end the war between the United States and Iran.

stories.headline: Swiss voters reject 10 million population cap
stories.summary: Nearly 55% of participants voted against the proposal to cap population by cutting migration, results show.

stories.headline: Swiss voters reject proposal to cap population at 10mn
stories.summary: Projections showed 54 per cent of voters rejected the initiative backed by the rightwing Swiss People’s party

stories.headline: Switzerland Narrowly Rejects Proposal to Cap Population at 10 Million
stories.summary: Switzerland recently held a referendum on a proposal to limit the country's population to 10 million, which was narrowly rejected by voters with a 55 to 45 percent margin. The proposal, primarily backed by the right-wing Swiss People's Party, aimed to restrict immigration amid concerns about space and sovereignty. Bloomberg News Switzerland Economy/Government reporter Bastian Benrath-Wright joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This

stories.headline: Trump says US-Iran deal to be signed on Sunday as Tehran casts doubt on timing
stories.summary: The US president's comments come as Iran says an exact date has not been decided.

stories.headline: Trump tells Israel and Hizbollah to ‘stand down’ as he pushes for Iran deal
stories.summary: US president has said he expects Washington and Tehran to seal a deal to reopen the Strait of Hormuz on Sunday

stories.headline: Swiss voters appear to reject proposal to cap population at 10 million
stories.summary: Far-right plan defeated in referendum, according to projection by national broadcaster SRFVoters in Switzerland appeared on Sunday to have rejected an unprecedented far-right proposal to cap the country’s population at 10 million in a divisive referendum dubbed “the Swiss Brexit”.A projection by ⁠the national broadcaster SRF, which traditionally calls ​referendum votes, showed that about 55% of voters were against the proposal by the Swiss People

stories.headline: Israel attacks Beirut on same day Trump says Iran deal to be signed
stories.summary: Israel has bombed the southern suburbs of Beirut, saying it targeted Hezbollah ‘infrastructure’.

stories.headline: Will the US-Iran deal be signed on Sunday? What we know so far
stories.summary: US says a deal to stop the war on Iran could be signed as early as Sunday. Tehran, however, disputes the timeline.

stories.headline: Swiss voters reject bid to cap population at 10 million, a move that would have limited immigration
stories.summary: A “yes” vote would have required the Swiss government to take action to cap the population by 2050, including limiting immigration.

stories.headline: Compressed Timeline for US-Iran Nuclear Deal Could Lead to Later Unraveling
stories.summary: Principal Deputy National Security Advisor under President Biden and former State Department Chief of Staff under President Obama Jonathan Finer joins David Gura and Christina Ruffini on Bloomberg This Weekend to discuss his concerns over the rushed timeline on such a complex issue that took the Obama administration two years to reach a deal on.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Ukraine and Moldova to enter first phase of EU membership negotiations
stories.summary: Election of new Hungarian government in April has paved way for EU member states to agree to open talks Ukraine and Moldova will take a decisive step towards joining the EU on Monday, as they embark on the first phase of membership negotiations.The start of substantive negotiations, launched by senior EU officials and ministers from both countries in Luxembourg on Monday, will be a highly symbolic moment for the two countries that were both part

stories.headline: UK forces seize suspected Russian oil tanker accused of funding Ukraine war

stories.headline: Trump condemns Israel’s attack on Beirut, says Iran deal still close
stories.summary: Donald Trump says there should be 'no more attacks' by Israel or Hezbollah on 'special day' when deal could be signed.

stories.headline: Israeli strikes hit Beirut as US-Iran talks near completion
stories.summary: Israeli jets struck Beirut's Dahiyeh district on Sunday, hitting the Ghobeiry area. Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu's office confirmed the strikes targeted Hezbollah infrastructure. Trump warns Israel and Iran not to 'blow it' after new strikes threaten emerging truce deal.

stories.headline: Romanian president nominates Adrian Vestea as prime minister
stories.summary: Romania has been embroiled in a renewed political crisis since its government collapsed in May following a no-confidence vote against Liberal Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan.

stories.headline: Deadly overnight Russian attack in Ukraine as industrial facilities were struck deep into Russia
stories.summary: Two killed and four injured in overnight Russian attacks on Ukraine's Sumy Oblast. On the same night, Ukrainian strikes hit strategic Russian industrial facilities located 700km from the border with Ukraine.

stories.headline: Trump Owns Shares in Company Hosting U.F.C. Bout as Part of His Vast Holdings

stories.headline: Trump at 80: A President ‘Really Uncomfortable’ With Aging

stories.headline: President Trump Endorses Mike Collins in Georgia Senate Runoff
stories.summary: Mr. Trump backed Mr. Collins over Derek Dooley, a former football coach supported by Gov. Brian Kemp, a Republican whose relationship with the president is strained.

stories.headline: Bloomberg This Weekend 6/14/2026
stories.summary: The news doesn’t stop when markets close. Hosts David Gura, Christina Ruffini and Lisa Mateo bring clarity, context and a bit of humor to the weekend’s biggest headlines, LIVE from New York. Joined by Sportico Deputy Editor Eben Novy- Williams, Fmr. Deputy National Security Advisor Jon Finer, New York Times Opinion Columnist Nicholas Kristkof, Maryland Rep. Johnny Olszewski, USA Today White House Correspondent Francesca Chambers, Associated Press

stories.headline: Lazard Undercuts Rival Centerview in Battle for Venezuela Deal
stories.summary: Investment bank Lazard Inc. is making a late bid to dislodge rival Centerview Partners as Venezuela’s financial adviser, offering to oversee one of the largest-ever sovereign debt restructurings for a sharply lower fee.

stories.headline: FIFA’s American Dream: How the World Cup Cashed In
stories.summary: The 2026 World Cup is set to generate more than $11 billion in revenue, making it the richest tournament in football history. But soaring ticket prices, dynamic pricing models, and a booming resale market are raising concerns that many longtime supporters are being priced out. Veteran England fan Andy Milne says attending his tenth World Cup will be by far his most expensive, while former Liverpool CEO Peter Moore argues that FIFA risks sacrifici

stories.headline: Equity Funding Squeeze Driven By AI Rally, SpaceX IPO Stretches Banks
stories.summary: The cost to fund US equity positions is surging unexpectedly, as a combination of the AI-driven stock rally, the massive growth of leveraged ETFs and SpaceX’s $75 billion public listing tests Wall Street’s market plumbing.

stories.headline: Diverging Rate Paths Force Reshuffle in Emerging-Market Bets
stories.summary: A growing divergence in interest-rate outlooks is forcing emerging-market investors to reshuffle bets.

stories.headline: South Korea’s World-Beating Stock Market Eyes Its MSCI Moment
stories.summary: After one of its most volatile weeks in years, South Korea’s stock market is approaching a milestone it has long been chasing: a potential path into MSCI Inc.’s developed-market status.

stories.headline: ‘Making history’: The fight to end female genital mutilation in Colombia
stories.summary: Colombia has passed the first legislation in Latin America to outlaw female genital mutilation. But barriers remain.

stories.headline: Bowen: Trump and Netanyahu wanted to reshape the Middle East - now they risk a permacrisis
